plan: Create the CardVisual component directory at src/ui/CardVisual/ with CardVisual.tsx and CardVisual.module.css

Implement CardVisual.tsx as a reusable presentational component that accepts a Card (from src/domain/card.ts) as a prop and renders: title (prompt), question text (guidance), category label, tier marker with color and text label, and flavour text (conditionally, only when present)

Style CardVisual.module.css to achieve a premium physical card look: rounded corners (border-radius), subtle shadow/elevation, readable hierarchy using existing design tokens from tokens.css (serif font, spacing scale, tier colors --color-tier-open/working/deep), tier color as a left border or top stripe with text label, flavour text in italic/muted treatment, responsive sizing for mobile/tablet/desktop breakpoints
Export CardVisual from src/ui/index.ts barrel file

Write unit tests in tests/unit/card-visual.test.tsx using React Testing Library: verify title renders, question text renders, category displays, tier marker displays with correct text label, flavour text renders when present, flavour text element is absent when empty string, semantic HTML structure (article or section role), accessibility (readable contrast via tier text labels not just color)
Run npm run lint, npm run typecheck, and npm run test to confirm all checks pass
testing_recommendations: Unit test: CardVisual renders the prompt field as the card title
Unit test: CardVisual renders the guidance field as question text
Unit test: CardVisual renders the category label
Unit test: CardVisual renders tier marker with text label (not color alone) for each tier: Open, Working, Deep
Unit test: CardVisual renders flavour text when flavourText is a non-empty string
Unit test: CardVisual does NOT render flavour text element when flavourText is empty string
Unit test: Tier marker has correct CSS class/data attribute for each tier value to verify color association
Unit test: Component uses semantic HTML (e.g. article element)
Run npm run lint to confirm no lint errors
Run npm run typecheck to confirm strict TypeScript compliance
Run npm run test to confirm all unit tests pass
risks: Card field naming: the Card type uses 'prompt' and 'guidance' rather than 'title' and 'question' — the component must map these correctly to the acceptance criteria (prompt = title, guidance = question text)
Flavour text conditional rendering must handle both undefined and empty string cases since flavourText is a required string field that may be empty in CSV data
Tier color accessibility — CLAUDE.md requires color must not be sole indicator, so tier marker must always include a text label alongside the color

Design decisions (card dimensions, shadow depth, typography hierarchy) are not specified — reasonable defaults using existing tokens.css values should be chosen, keeping the premium/tactile feel described in CLAUDE.md
